FULL WIRING GUIDE v1.0
⚡ WIRING INSTRUCTIONS
ESP32 Smart Waste Sorter · Complete Circuit Reference
🗺️ Full Circuit Diagram
🖱️ Hover to zoom · Right-click → Save image to download ESP32 Smart Waste Sorter
⚠️
VOLTAGE DIVIDER REQUIRED The inductive proximity sensor (LJ12A3) output is 12V. You MUST use a 20kΩ + 10kΩ voltage divider before GPIO 13 to bring it down to ~3V. Connecting 12V directly to ESP32 GPIO will permanently damage the chip!
✅
COMMON GROUND REQUIRED All power supplies (USB 5V and 2S 18650 battery pack) must share a common GND connection. Connect all GND wires together on the breadboard GND rail.
🎨 Wire Color Legend
RED — VCC / Power (5V or 7.4V)
BLACK — GND / Ground
BLUE — Stepper coil signals
ORANGE — Stepper coil signals
GREEN — Sensor signal / ECHO lines
PURPLE — Servo signal wire
CYAN — Ultrasonic TRIG lines
YELLOW — Analog sensor output
⚡ Power Wiring
From To Wire Notes
18650 Battery (+) KCD11 Switch IN
Main power line
KCD11 Switch OUT LM2596 Buck IN(+)
Switched power to buck
LM2596 Buck OUT(+) Breadboard VCC rail
Set to 5V with potentiometer
18650 Battery (−) Breadboard GND rail
Common ground
ESP32 GND Breadboard GND rail
Must share common GND
1000µF Capacitor (+) Buck OUT (+) / VCC rail
Smoothing capacitor
1000µF Capacitor (−) Breadboard GND rail
Capacitor ground
📡 Sensor Wiring
Sensor Pin Connects To Notes
👁️ IR Sensor VCC 3.3V or 5V rail Power
GND GND rail Ground
OUT GPIO 32 Object present = LOW
💧 Rain Sensor VCC 3.3V rail Power
GND GND rail Ground
AO GPIO 35 Analog input-only pin
🔩 Inductive Sensor VCC Battery+ (7.4V direct) ⚠ Needs 6–36V
GND GND rail (common) ⚠ Must share GND!
OUT 20kΩ resistor ⚠ Voltage divider first!
DIV GPIO 13 After 20kΩ+10kΩ divider → ~3V
📡 HC-SR04 Wet VCC 5V rail Power
TRIG GPIO 26 Trigger pulse output
ECHO GPIO 12 via 10kΩ+20kΩ ⚠ 5V echo → use divider!
📡 HC-SR04 Dry VCC 5V rail Power
TRIG GPIO 25 Trigger pulse output
ECHO GPIO 14 via 10kΩ+20kΩ ⚠ 5V echo → use divider!
📡 HC-SR04 Metal VCC 5V rail Power
TRIG GPIO 33 Trigger pulse output
ECHO GPIO 27 via 10kΩ+20kΩ ⚠ 5V echo → use divider!
⚙️ Actuator Wiring
Actuator Pin Connects To Notes
⚙️ 28BYJ-48 Stepper VCC 5V rail ULN2003 board power
GNDGND railGround
IN1GPIO 4Coil 1
IN2GPIO 2Coil 2
IN3GPIO 18Coil 3
IN4GPIO 19Coil 4
🔄 SG90 Servo RED 5V rail Power
BROWNGND railGround
ORANGEGPIO 23PWM signal
🔔 Active Buzzer VCC 3.3V or 5V Power
GNDGND railGround
I/OGPIO 21Active-LOW = ON
⚠️ Voltage Divider Wiring
Signal R1 R2 GPIO Result
🔩 Inductive Sensor OUT (12V) 20kΩ 10kΩ GPIO 13 ~3.0V ✅ Safe
📡 HC-SR04 ECHO (5V) 10kΩ 20kΩ ECHO pins ~3.3V ✅ Safe
Inductive Sensor OUT ──[20kΩ]──┬── GPIO 13
                                    └──[10kΩ]── GND

HC-SR04 ECHO ──[10kΩ]──┬── GPIO (12/14/27)
                            └──[20kΩ]── GND
🚀 Assembly Order
STEP 01
⚡ Set Up Power Rails
Connect batteries → switch → LM2596 buck converter. Set output to 5V using the potentiometer. Add 1000µF capacitor across output rails.
STEP 02
🧠 Place ESP32
Place ESP32 NodeMCU across the centre of the breadboard. Connect GND to GND rail and VIN/5V to VCC rail.
STEP 03
⚙️ Wire Stepper Motor
Connect ULN2003 board VCC/GND to rails. Wire IN1→4, IN2→2, IN3→18, IN4→19 to ESP32.
STEP 04
🔄 Wire Servo
Red → 5V, Brown → GND, Orange signal wire → GPIO 23.
STEP 05
📡 Wire Ultrasonic Sensors
Each HC-SR04: VCC→5V, GND→GND, TRIG→GPIO, ECHO→GPIO via 10kΩ+20kΩ voltage divider.
STEP 06
👁️ Wire IR Sensor
VCC→3.3V, GND→GND, OUT→GPIO 32 directly. No divider needed.
STEP 07
💧 Wire Rain Sensor
VCC→3.3V, GND→GND, AO (analog out) → GPIO 35. Use AO not DO.
STEP 08
🔩 Wire Metal Detector
VCC → battery 7.4V direct. GND → common GND rail. OUT → 20kΩ → GPIO 13 → 10kΩ → GND.
STEP 09
🔔 Wire Buzzer
VCC→5V, GND→GND, I/O signal → GPIO 21. Active-LOW — buzzer ON when pin is LOW.
STEP 10
✅ Test & Verify
Upload sketch, open Serial Monitor at 115200 baud. Confirm WiFi connects and ThingSpeak returns code 200.
